what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-source channel, linking attachment id att-70e1e6af to the image target image://design-source/capital-raise/p-05.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
target references an image URI under design-source/capital-raise/p-05
standouts: Attachment metadata acts as a pointer/ledger entry to an external image asset
Channel 'design-source' and capital-raise path suggest a design artifact tied to fundraising material
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Target URI uses an image:// scheme with a channel/path structure
concerns: No descriptive title, caption, or alt text for the image
notes: This file is a minimal ledger stub that points to an image asset in the capital-raise design folder.
questions: What image does p-05 represent and what is its role in the capital-raise design set?
Is the target URI resolvable and versioned?
Should there be a caption or description field?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (attachment metadata) matches actual content: it records an image attachment reference.
intention_signals: To create a durable, channel-scoped ledger entry that links a design-source conversation attachment to a specific image asset for later retrieval and context.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme to address channel-scoped image assets.
contribution: Provides a stable attachment record that maps an attachment identifier to an external image resource within a channel namespace.
summary_for_embedding: YAML metadata file att-70e1e6af.md in the design-source channel that references image asset image://design-source/capital-raise/p-05.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog an image attachment so it can be retrieved and associated with design-source channel context.
